ksa_text: Establish and maintain the enterprise AI inventory, cataloging all AI systems in production with risk tier, owner, training data provenance, model version, and review status, ensuring all applications are registered and subject to appropriate review and monitoring.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ensure that AI design and development activities are properly documented and updated.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Capability building for digital, data and technology
Ensure that AI design and development activities are properly documented and updated.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: AI Governance falls outside the primary competency scope of the Data Platform Team Lead. This task KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is AI Governance truly out-of-scope for the Data Platform Team Lead? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list AI Governance as a core or recommended competency for the Data Platform Team Lead. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms AI Governance falls outside the established competency profile for the Data Platform Team Lead. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: AI Governance is outside the competency scope of the Data Platform Team Lead. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
